title: Quiz: Hardware Implementations of Quantum Reservoirs
description: Test your understanding of quantum reservoir hardware platforms and their characteristics

questions.question: What is a key hardware requirement specific to quantum reservoir computing?
questions.explanation: QRC leverages natural quantum dynamics as the reservoir, unlike gate-based QC which requires precise control. The system's inherent Hamiltonian evolution provides the nonlinear transformation needed for computation.

questions.question: What physical effect in superconducting qubits limits QRC evolution time?
questions.explanation: Superconducting qubits experience T1 (energy relaxation) and T2 (dephasing) decoherence, which limit how long quantum information can be preserved. Typical T2 times are 10-100 μs.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of photonic QRC systems?

options.text: Long coherence times at low temperatures

questions.explanation: Photonic systems operate at room temperature and have extremely fast dynamics (optical frequencies), making them suitable for high-speed data processing applications.

questions.question: In NMR quantum reservoirs, what provides the natural coupling between spins?
questions.explanation: NMR systems use J-coupling (scalar coupling), which arises from electron-mediated interactions between nuclear spins through chemical bonds. This provides fixed, natural coupling topology.

questions.question: Readout error mitigation is unnecessary in QRC because reservoir computing is inherently noise-robust.
questions.explanation: False. While QRC has some noise tolerance, readout errors directly affect the extracted features used for training. Error mitigation techniques like calibration-based correction improve performance.

questions.question: Which platform offers the longest coherence times?
questions.explanation: NMR systems typically have the longest coherence times (T2 can be hundreds of milliseconds to seconds in liquid NMR), though they have other limitations like low polarization and slow operations.

questions.question: What noise-robust QRC strategy involves using noise level to set regularization?
questions.explanation: Noise-aware ridge regression sets the regularization parameter proportional to the estimated noise level. This prevents overfitting to noisy measurements while still extracting useful signal.

questions.question: Which factors should be considered when selecting a QRC hardware platform? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Platform selection should consider: (a) data rate compatibility with operation speed, (b) task requirements that favor certain architectures, and (c) practical constraints like temperature. The lab aesthetics are not relevant.
